text.title: Refactoring Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns that defeat the two-hat rule or expand refactor scope beyond review tolerance.

antipattern.symptom: One PR / commit both restructures and adds new behaviour. Tests change semantically (asserting new outputs). Reviewer cannot tell intent from diff.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wants to "tidy" while implementing and bundles both. The cost is paid by reviewer, who cannot separate refactor from feature, and by bisect which can no longer attribute regressions.
antipattern.fix: Split into two commits: refactor first (tests pass identically), feature second (tests change). CI gate: `refactor:` commit cannot modify the test files.

antipattern.symptom: IDE-driven rename completes silently; misses string-key references (template literals, dynamic getattr, ORM `select_related('old_name')`).
antipattern.root-cause: Language-server refactor is AST-scoped — it does not touch strings unless the language has tagged-template support. Dynamic lookups by name string slip through.
antipattern.fix: After IDE rename, run `git grep '\\bold_name\\b'` and fix matches manually. Add a unit test exercising the renamed dynamic path BEFORE rename.

antipattern.symptom: Extract Method produces a one-call-site helper named `helper1` / `_internal_x`. Indirection added; clarity reduced; no second call site materialises.
antipattern.root-cause: Extracting on first occurrence creates speculative abstraction; the second occurrence almost always differs from what was extracted, requiring a re-design.
antipattern.fix: Rule of Three: do not extract until the third duplicate appears. If a single big function needs splitting for readability — split, but name parts by intent (`validate_payment`, not `helper1`).

antipattern.symptom: `refactor:` commit touches 30+ files. Reviewer marks "LGTM" without reading; regressions land.
antipattern.root-cause: Author rolls multiple refactors into one branch; commit boundary disappears. Reviewer cognitive load exceeds capacity.
antipattern.fix: Enforce `templates/refactor-scope-guard.sh` as pre-commit hook. Cap at 5 files per refactor commit; chain commits if more touched.
